title: T6: AngelEye MCP wrapper — read-only stdio surface over the existing API
prompt: AngelEye (/Users/davidcruwys/dev/ad/apps/angeleye, Express read API on :5051 per its .env — NOT the 5500/5501 its CLAUDE.md claims) has no MCP surface; this is the first retrofit under the every-app-exposes-both API+MCP doctrine. Build a thin READ-ONLY stdio MCP server at server/src/expose/mcp.ts that proxies the existing HTTP API (health, sessions, search, session_events, session_enrichments, projects, diagnostics — curl-pin each endpoint first, drop dead peripherals) following the appyradar-sentinel src/expose/mcp.ts pattern (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk, StdioServerTransport, stdout protocol-only, graceful error when the server is down via an ANGELEYE_MCP_BASE_URL override for testing). Prove it with a piped JSON-RPC initialize/tools-list/tools-call handshake plus a dead-port negative, register it user-scope as 'angeleye' via `claude mcp add` with absolute repo-local tsx paths, document the surface in the AngelEye repo (docs/mcp-surface.md + a CLAUDE.md section), commit and push in the AngelEye repo, and self-report to engine/store/results/. Never restart or kill a running AngelEye server; if it is down start it only via ./scripts/start.sh (Overmind), and if it will not boot, park — that is T6-04 revival territory, not yours. Read and execute the war game at backlog/wargames/T6-05-angeleye-mcp.md. Follow its moves in order; on any failure signal apply the counter-move; on any abort condition stop and write needs-decision/.
verify: server/src/expose/mcp.ts exists in the angeleye repo; `npm run typecheck -w server` exits 0 there; @modelcontextprotocol/sdk is in server/package.json; re-running the war game's Move 4 piped JSON-RPC handshake prints 'handshake OK' with >=5 tools listed and real session data returned, and the dead-port negative prints 'not reachable'; `claude mcp get angeleye` shows the registration with absolute tsx + mcp.ts paths; docs/mcp-surface.md exists and CLAUDE.md gained an 'MCP surface' section in the angeleye repo; the work is committed (message contains wg-t6-05) and pushed; engine/store/results/ contains the wg-t6-05 self-report. Negative: the legacy appyradar-sentinal MCP entry in ~/.claude.json is untouched and no other mcpServers entry changed; no diff to angeleye's server/src/index.ts, routes/, or .env; the AngelEye server on :5051 was never killed (still listening after the run if it was up or was started).
